_comment: Golden analysis fixture: two short, hand-picked games with known engine outcomes, for the IMP-ANLZ end-to-end test. 'alice' is the tracked player (me). Game 1: alice wins by Scholar's mate (opponent-side blunder into mate lacks a unique reply, so nothing is flagged — a clean win). Game 2: black hangs its queen with 2...Qh4 and alice MISSES it (3.Nc3 instead of the uniquely-winning Nxh4), then black retreats and the game is drawn — a flagged mistake with a distinctly-best answer, so it also becomes a trainer puzzle. Kept tiny so real Stockfish analysis runs in a second.

games.pgn:
[Event "Live Chess"]
[Site "Chess.com"]
[White "alice"]
[Black "bob"]
[Result "1-0"]
[ECO "C20"]
[TimeControl "180"]
[Termination "alice won by checkmate"]

1. e4 {[%clk 0:03:00]} e5 {[%clk 0:02:58]} 2. Qh5 {[%clk 0:02:55]} Nc6 {[%clk 0:02:50]} 3. Bc4 {[%clk 0:02:52]} Nf6 {[%clk 0:02:40]} 4. Qxf7# {[%clk 0:02:50]} 1-0

[Event "Live Chess"]
[Site "Chess.com"]
[White "alice"]
[Black "carol"]
[Result "1/2-1/2"]
[ECO "C20"]
[TimeControl "180"]
[Termination "Game drawn by agreement"]

1. e4 {[%clk 0:03:00]} e5 {[%clk 0:02:58]} 2. Nf3 {[%clk 0:02:52]} Qh4 {[%clk 0:02:50]} 3. Nc3 {[%clk 0:02:45]} Qe7 {[%clk 0:02:40]} 4. Bc4 {[%clk 0:02:38]} Nf6 {[%clk 0:02:30]} 1/2-1/2
